_comment: Curated list of mixin handler methods that FATALLY crash on the target MC and
cannot be repaired by remapping. Retromod surgically removes the listed handler
method(s) from the mixin during transformation, so the mixin framework never
processes them — the mod loads and the one feature that handler implemented is
inert, instead of the whole game crashing.
This is the escape hatch for failures we cannot safely auto-detect — most notably
from a vanilla method whose local-variable layout changed between MC versions. The
@Local then resolves to the wrong slot and MixinExtras emits an invalid bridge
(VerifyError: Bad local variable type) at class-load time.
Users can extend or override this via config/retromod/mixin-blocklist.json (same
format). Entries from both files are merged.
Format: each entry needs a 'mixin' (the mixin class, '/' or '.' separated). If
'methods' is given, only those handler methods are stripped (surgical, preferred).
If 'methods' is omitted/empty, every injector handler on the class is stripped.

blocked.reason: MixinExtras @WrapOperation + @Local Level on HangingEntityItem.useOn, wrapping ItemStack.shrink(I). MC 26.1 restructured useOn, so the @Local Level resolves to a non-existent slot and MixinExtras emits an invalid bridge (VerifyError: Bad local variable type) that crashes at bootstrap. Stripping just this handler lets Deeper and Darker boot; the painting stack-decrement tweak is inert.

@Inject handlers on Entity.addAdditionalSaveData/readAdditionalSaveData capture CompoundTag, but MC moved those params to ValueOutput/ValueInput in the 1.21.5 serialization (ValueIO/codec) refactor -> InvalidInjectionException (Invalid descriptor: expected ValueOutput, found CompoundTag) on 1.21.11. Not a rename (ValueOutput has a different API than CompoundTag), so the handler body can't be auto-retyped yet. Stripping these handlers removes the injection error. NOTE (#48): Darker Depths' death-anchor feature is interdependent (the stripped handlers leave orphaned helper lambdas + a @Unique field/interface), so this mod still does not fully construct after the strip — it's a PARTIAL soft-fail, kept here because the handlers are genuinely broken and the strip is correct as far as it goes. Mods with a self-contained broken handler soft-fail cleanly.
